findings.evidence: Statement at line 761 is `lambda • Rlx - generatorMap Rlx_dom = x`, i.e. (λ−A)R(λ)x = x; no lemma in the file proves R(λ)(λ−A) = I or injectivity.
findings.fix: Restrict the claim to the right-inverse identity; drop or explicitly mark as future work the 'bounded inverse' and '(0,∞) ⊂ ρ(A)' statements.
findings.issue: resolventRightInv proves only the right inverse `(λI−A)R(λ)x = x`, but its docstring claims this 'shows R_λ is the bounded inverse of λI − A on all of X, establishing that (0,∞) ⊂ ρ(A)'. The left inverse / injectivity needed for a genuine inverse and for membership in the resolvent set is not proven here (and is deferred per the PR description).

findings.fix: Keep the result statement; move proof-strategy text into proof comments, leaving docstrings to describe what each declaration is.
findings.issue: Public docstrings describe the proof rather than the object/result: existsGrowthBound ('The proof uses the uniform bound M...'), strongContAt ('Left continuity uses the operator norm bound...'), hilleYosidaResolventBound ('essentially free from the construction... follows from LinearMap.mkContinuous_norm_le'), generatorMap ('Uses Classical.choose...'), domain ('algebraically closed... because limits of sums...').

summary: Module and per-declaration documentation is present and mostly accurate, but the resolventRightInv docstring overclaims a two-sided inverse / resolvent-set result that the statement (right inverse only) does not deliver, and several public docstrings narrate the proof instead of describing the result.
